description: Official ESPHome Starter Kit by Apollo Automation. Plug-and-play kit with an ESP32-C6-MINI-1 module, two 14-pin FPC expansion ports, onboard RGB LED, and USB-C power. Comes with four swappable modules: Motion Module (MH-SR602, 0-3.5 m range), AHT20F Temperature and Humidity Module, an RGB + Buzzer Module (10 RGB LEDs and a piezo buzzer in the ESPHome Casita form factor), and a Button Module. Up to two modules can be used simultaneously to build a multisensor. No soldering or coding required.

featured_bundles.description:
The starter kit's RGB + Buzzer module. 10 RGB LEDs across the front.
On the back: a piezo buzzer plus the FPC connector that plugs into
the board. Adding this drops the LEDs, the buzzer, and the ringtone
player into your config all at once.
featured_bundles.name: RGB LED + Buzzer Module

featured_components.description:
The shared I²C bus for the kit's sensor modules. Configure it once
and the AHT20 module uses it, along with Apollo's optional breakout
boards for adding more I²C sensors.
featured_components.name: I²C Bus (sensor modules)

featured_components.description:
Temperature and humidity module shipped with the starter kit. Plug
it into either FPC connector and add the I²C Bus component so it
can talk to the board. Accurate to ±0.3°C and ±2% humidity, useful
for tracking room comfort or humidity in places like a bathroom or
basement.
featured_components.name: AHT20 Temperature & Humidity Module

featured_components.description:
Onboard fuel gauge for monitoring the LiPo battery's voltage and
charge level.
featured_components.name: Battery Fuel Gauge (MAX17048)

featured_components.description:
Internal switch that powers the FPC accessory connectors at boot.
You don't need to touch this. It's set to always-on so plug-in
modules get power before they start up.
featured_components.name: Accessory Power Rail

featured_components.description:
Motion sensor module included with the starter kit. Detects motion
in rooms like a kitchen, bathroom, or hallway, perfect for
triggering lights or other automations.
featured_components.name: Motion Module (MH-SR602)

featured_components.description:
The physical button on the dev board. Use it as a secondary button
in automations, or hold it during power-on to enter boot mode for
flashing.
featured_components.name: Boot Button (onboard)

featured_components.description:
Pushbutton module shipped with the starter kit. Plug it into the
FPC connector (GPIO 6) and use it as a button input in automations.
featured_components.name: Button Module

featured_components.description:
The 10 RGB LEDs by themselves, without the buzzer parts. If you have
the whole RGB + Buzzer module, add the "RGB LED + Buzzer Module"
bundle instead. It includes the LEDs along with the buzzer and
ringtone player.
featured_components.name: RGB LEDs (module)

featured_components.description: The onboard RGB LED on the ESP32-C6 module.
featured_components.name: Onboard RGB LED

featured_components.description:
The piezo buzzer output by itself, without the LEDs. If you have
the whole RGB + Buzzer module, add the "RGB LED + Buzzer Module"
bundle instead. It includes the buzzer along with the LEDs and
ringtone player.
featured_components.name: Piezo Buzzer (module)

featured_components.description:
Plays ringtones through a piezo buzzer (RTTTL format). If you have
the whole RGB + Buzzer module, add the "RGB LED + Buzzer Module"
bundle instead. It includes the ringtone player along with the
LEDs and buzzer.
featured_components.name: RTTTL Player (buzzer)

name: Apollo ESPHome Starter Kit

pins.label: I2C SCL
pins.notes: I2C clock line for sensor modules

pins.label: I2C SDA
pins.notes: I2C data line for sensor modules

pins.label: ACCESSORY PWR
pins.notes: Internal GPIO switch, ALWAYS_ON with setup_priority 2000 — must latch high before FPC modules enumerate
pins.occupied_by: Accessory power switch (controls FPC module power rail)

pins.label: ONBOARD LED
pins.notes: WS2812 addressable LED, active by default
